title: phyllanthus boehmii
intro: Phyllanthus boehmii, also known as Galphimia glauca, is a plant species native to Mexico and Central America, belonging to the family Phyllanthaceae. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antispasmodic, and antiseptic effects. Its health benefits include treating conditions such as fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, as well as reducing inflammation and relieving pain. The plant can be prepared as a herbal tea, tincture, or infusion, and is also used in traditional remedies to treat various ailments, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy.
